content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me examine atom C:6 (carbon with index 6) more carefully to identify its neighboring atoms.

From the image, I can clearly see that atom C:6 is connected to:
- N:7 (nitrogen, index 7) - blue sphere directly bonded to C:6
- N:12 (nitrogen, index 12) - blue sphere directly bonded to C:6  
- C:1 (carbon, index 1) - yellow/orange sphere directly bonded to C:6
- C:10 (carbon, index 10) - yellow/orange sphere directly bonded to C:6

Looking at the molecular structure, atom C:6 appears to be part of a ring system and has exactly 4 covalent bonds as expected for a carbon atom in this context.

The geomconf_index for this molecule can be determined from the filename "geomconf_4823.xyz", which indicates the geomconf_index is 4823.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:6 are:
- Atom index 7, element N
- Atom index 12, element N  
- Atom index 1, element C
- Atom index 10, element C

And the geomconf_index is 4823.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 6, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
